note: Mathlib/Logic/Relation.lean:896-898 theorem church_rosser: 'A sufficient condition for the Church-Rosser property' -- an abstract-rewriting-system (any relation r satisfying local confluence) generalization of the classical theorem, which is specifically about lambda-calculus beta-reduction; Mathlib has no lambda calculus, so this is the generic confluence machinery the concept specializes.

Verified key exists in catalog/data/hierarchy.json (libraries.Mathlib.modules.Probability.sub.Process, 365 decls: Filtration.lean, Adapted.lean, Predictable.lean, Stopping.lean, HittingTime.lean, Kolmogorov.lean) plus sibling Mathlib/Probability/Martingale (209 decls) and Mathlib/Probability/BrownianMotion -- together the closest existing home for the foundational objects of stochastic calculus (filtered probability spaces, adapted/predictable processes, stopping times, martingales, Brownian motion). No Itô integral / SDE theory yet, so this is infrastructure not the calculus itself -- medium confidence, field-level container only.

Mathlib/Analysis/SpecialFunctions/Trigonometric/Cotangent.lean:236-240 theorem cot_series_rep, in `section MittagLeffler`, docstring explicitly calls the cotangent partial-fraction expansion 'also known as the Mittag-Leffler expansion' -- this proves ONE specific instance (cot's poles) rather than the general Mittag-Leffler theorem (existence of a meromorphic fn with arbitrary prescribed principal parts), so related not exact/special_case.

Mathlib/Combinatorics/SimpleGraph/Walk/Chord.lean:44-46 def IsChordless: 'a walk with no chords' -- the primitive chord/chordless-walk vocabulary a chordal-graph definition would be built from (chordal = every cycle of length >=4 has a chord), but the graph-level property (no long induced/chordless cycle) itself is not defined anywhere in Mathlib -- grep for 'Chordal' across Mathlib returns nothing.
